name: dotnet-episteme-skills
description: Practical, production-tested .NET skills that provide enterprise-proven instructions for AI-assisted development.

skills.name: dotnet-inspect
skills.description: Use when inspecting NuGet APIs, listing public types, or decompiling method signatures.

skills.name: code-review
skills.description: Use for PR/diff/branch .NET reviews: correctness, performance, security, data, messaging, observability.

skills.name: dotnet-techne-crap-analysis
skills.description: Use for CRAP score, complexity, and coverage hotspot analysis to prioritise risk reduction.

skills.name: dotnet-techne-csharp-api-design
skills.description: Use for public API design changes requiring compatibility-safe versioning and deprecation strategy.

skills.name: dotnet-techne-csharp-coding-standards
skills.description: Use for modern C# coding standards, refactoring guidance, and maintainable performance-focused code.

skills.name: dotnet-techne-csharp-concurrency-patterns
skills.description: Use for choosing async/channels/dataflow/Rx patterns and avoiding lock-heavy concurrency bugs.

skills.name: dotnet-techne-csharp-type-design-performance
skills.description: Use for type/collection choices on hot paths: readonly structs, sealed types, spans, frozen collections.

skills.name: dotnet-techne-serialisation
skills.description: Use for serialisation format and contract decisions across APIs, messaging, and persistence.
